title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard framework that enables AI agents and applications to communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports multiple transport mechanisms including STDIO and HTTP, allowing seamless integration across AI frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude.
writeup: A ground-up introduction to the Model Context Protocol (MCP) that builds the case through two concrete pain points: the explosion of bespoke API integration code in AI apps, and the inability to reuse that code across agentic frameworks such as LangGraph, OpenAI Agent SDK, and CrewAI. MCP resolves both by introducing a uniform client/server split where the AI app embeds only a thin MCP client that speaks JSON-RPC, while MCP servers encapsulate all integration complexity for any external resource, including REST APIs, databases, local files, GitHub, and internal systems. The through-line is portability and separation of concerns: one MCP server can be reused unchanged across every framework, and any number of servers can be composed inside a single multi-agent application.

similar.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
Automate project intake with multi-agent AI using MCP, Google ADK, Cloud Run, and BigQuery
The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
Building Interactive UIs in VS Code with MCP Apps — Marlene Mhangami & Liam Hampton, GitHub
From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data

key_points: Without MCP, each external service (weather, Google Drive, GitHub, PostgreSQL) requires its own bespoke integration code written to that API's request and response format, and that code must be rewritten from scratch for every agentic framework (LangGraph, OpenAI Agent SDK, CrewAI, Agno) the developer adopts.

MCP splits the integration problem into two roles: an MCP client embedded in the AI application that handles only connection establishment, and an MCP server that owns all API authentication, request formatting, and response parsing, leaving the AI app with minimal integration surface.

All MCP communication travels over JSON-RPC, and this protocol is identical regardless of which agentic framework hosts the MCP client, making MCP servers portable and reusable across LangGraph, OpenAI Agent SDK, CrewAI, and Agno without modification.

In a multi-agent application each agent connects to its own MCP server (for example, a Tavily web-search server, a weather server, a GitHub server, and a Maps server), so the application avoids per-API authentication and documentation overhead for each agent separately.

MCP servers are not limited to REST APIs: the protocol covers local files, Google Drive, GitHub repositories, operating system tools, relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL), and internal company systems, making it a general-purpose external-resource interface for AI applications.
